back_translation: When the day of Pentecost arrived, they all were together in one place.
Suddenly, there was a sound from the sky like the sound of a powerful storm rushing violently, resounding throughout the house where they were sitting.
Something appeared to them resembling flames of fire like tongues, dividing and spreading out to rest upon each of them.
All were filled to overflowing with the Holy Spirit, and began to speak various languages, according as the Spirit graciously enabled them to utter forth.
In Jerusalem there were Jews, God-fearing people, dwelling, having come from every nation under the sky.
When that sound rang out, the crowd gathered together and was bewildered and puzzled, because each one heard them speaking his own language.
They were startled and amazed, and said, "Behold, are not all these people who are speaking Galileans?
Then why do each of us hear them speaking in our own regional language that we have learned from birth?
People of Parthia, people of Media, people of Elam, those dwelling in Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, the province of Asia,
Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, and the region of Libya near the city of Cyrene, together with visitors from the city of Rome,
both Jews and those who have converted to enter the Jewish religion, people of Crete and Arabs—we hear them proclaiming the great royal deeds of God in our own languages!"
Everyone was startled and thoroughly puzzled, and asked each other, "What does this mean?"
But others mocked them, saying, "These people are just drunk on new wine!"

Peter stood up together with the other eleven apostles, lifted his voice and said to them, "Fellow brothers of Judea and all those dwelling in the city of Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and incline your ears to listen to my words.
These people are not drunk as you suppose, for it is only the third hour of the day.
But this event is what God has spoken through the prophet Joel, saying,
'In the last age, God says, I will pour out my Spirit upon all humankind. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.
Even upon my male servants and my female servants, in those days I will pour out my Spirit upon them, and they will prophesy.'
'I will manifest wonders in the sky above, and signs on the earth below—blood, fire, and columns of smoke.
The sun will become darkness, the moon will become blood, before the day of the Lord, great and glorious, arrives.
And everyone who calls out the name of the Lord will receive salvation.'

Fellow Israelites, listen to these words. Jesus of Nazareth was one whom God certified to you by mighty miracles and various signs which God performed through Him in your midst, as you yourselves know.
This one was handed over according to the firmly-determined plan and foreknowledge of God; you nailed and executed Him by the hands of lawless men.
But God raised Him back to life, loosing Him from the pains of death, because death could not hold Him.
For David spoke concerning Him, saying, 'I saw the Lord before me continually; He dwells at my right side so that I will not waver.
Therefore my heart rejoices, my tongue exults; moreover, my flesh will dwell in hope,
because You will not abandon my soul in the realm of death, and You will not allow Your holy one to experience decay.
You have revealed the paths of life to me; You will fill me with joy when I am in Your presence.'
Brothers and sisters, let me speak boldly to you concerning David our forefather: he died and was buried, and his tomb remains with us to this day.
But David was a prophet and knew that God had sworn to him that He would establish one of his descendants to sit on his throne.
David saw in advance and therefore spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that He was not abandoned in the realm of death, and His flesh did not experience decay.
God raised this Jesus back to life, of which we are witnesses.
Therefore, when He was exalted to the right side of God, and received the Holy Spirit according to the promise from the Father, He poured out the Spirit as you now see and hear.
For David did not ascend to heaven, but he himself said, 'The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side,
until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.'
Therefore let all the lineage of Israel know with certainty that God has established this Jesus, whom you crucified, as both Lord and Christ."
When they heard this, they felt pierced to the heart, and said to Peter and the other apostles, "Brothers, what shall we do?"
Peter said to them, "Repent, and let each of you receive baptism in the name of Jesus Christ so that your sins may be forgiven, and then you will receive the Holy Spirit as a gift.
For this promise belongs to you and your children and descendants, and to all who are far away—all whom the Lord our God will call to Himself."
Peter further testified and urged them with many other words, saying, "Receive deliverance from this crooked generation."
Those who received his words received baptism; on that day about three thousand people joined them.
They devoted themselves to the teaching of the apostles, fellowship, the breaking of bread, and prayer.
Everyone experienced reverent fear, and many miracles and signs occurred through the apostles.
The believers stayed together and shared everything with one another.
They sold their property and possessions and distributed to everyone according to each person's need.
Every day they unanimously devoted themselves at the temple, and broke bread from house to house, eating meals together with joyful exultation and sincerity of heart,
praising God and being well-regarded by all the people. And the Lord added those who were being saved to their number daily.
